title: Inverted sort order when using DISTINCT and a descending index
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(y INTEGER, z VARCHAR, a DOUBLE, b BOOLEAN);

INSERT INTO t2 VALUES(3, 'apple', 1.1, TRUE), (1, 'banana', 2.2, FALSE), (5, 'cherry', 3.3, TRUE), (2, 'date', 4.4, FALSE), (6, 'fig', 5.5, TRUE), (4, 'grape', 6.6, FALSE), (5, 'honeydew', 7.7, TRUE), (1, 'kiwi', 8.8, FALSE), (3, 'lemon', 9.9, TRUE);
CREATE INDEX t2y ON t2(y DESC);
SELECT DISTINCT y FROM t2 ORDER BY y ASC;
SELECT DISTINCT z FROM t2 WHERE a > 2.0 ORDER BY z DESC;
key_bug_features: DISTINCT query with descending index returns incorrect sort order
